feed.title: Wasil Zafar — Psychology
feed.subtitle: Latest psychology articles from Wasil Zafar

entry.title: Foundations of Social Psychology
entry.summary: Explore the foundations of social psychology — learn what it is, how it differs from other disciplines, key research methods, classic experiments like Milgram and Asch, and ethical guidelines.

entry.title: The Self-Concept & Identity
entry.summary: Explore the self-concept in social psychology — understand actual, ideal, and ought selves, self-discrepancy theory, identity formation, impression management, and cultural influences on self-construal.

entry.title: Self-Esteem & Self-Perception
entry.summary: Master self-esteem and self-perception in social psychology — contingent self-worth, narcissism, Bem

entry.title: Social Cognition
entry.summary: Master social cognition — learn how schemas, heuristics, and cognitive biases shape the way we perceive, interpret, and remember information about other people and social situations.

entry.title: Attribution Theory
entry.summary: Master attribution theory in social psychology — learn how we explain behavior through internal and external causes, the fundamental attribution error, actor-observer bias, and self-serving attributions.

entry.title: Cognitive Dissonance
entry.summary: Master cognitive dissonance theory — Leon Festinger

entry.title: Conformity & Obedience
entry.summary: Explore conformity and obedience in social psychology — Asch

entry.title: Compliance & Persuasion
entry.summary: Master compliance techniques and persuasion psychology — Cialdini

entry.title: Social Influence in Groups
entry.summary: Explore social influence within groups — social facilitation, social loafing, minority influence, emotional contagion, and how the presence of others impacts individual performance and behavior.

entry.title: Social Identity Theory
entry.summary: Master Social Identity Theory — Henri Tajfel

entry.title: Stereotypes, Prejudice & Discrimination
entry.summary: Understand stereotypes, prejudice, and discrimination — cognitive foundations, emotional prejudice, behavioral discrimination, implicit bias, the Implicit Association Test, and modern forms of racism.

entry.title: Stereotype Threat & Reducing Prejudice
entry.summary: Learn about stereotype threat and evidence-based strategies for reducing prejudice — contact hypothesis, cooperative learning, shared identity, Robbers Cave experiment, and recategorization.

entry.title: Group Decision Making & Groupthink
entry.summary: Understand group decision making and groupthink — Irving Janis

entry.title: Deindividuation & Bystander Effect
entry.summary: Explore deindividuation and the bystander effect — how anonymity in crowds changes behavior, diffusion of responsibility, Kitty Genovese, leadership in groups, and strategies for conflict resolution.

entry.title: Attraction & Relationships
entry.summary: Explore the psychology of attraction and relationships — proximity, similarity, physical attractiveness, Sternberg

entry.title: Aggression & Prosocial Behavior
entry.summary: Explore aggression and prosocial behavior — biological and social causes of aggression, frustration-aggression hypothesis, media violence, altruism, empathy-altruism hypothesis, and helping behavior.

entry.title: Culture, Socialization & Media
entry.summary: Explore how culture, socialization, and media shape social behavior — individualism vs collectivism, agents of socialization, social media psychology, echo chambers, and digital identity.

entry.title: Applied Social Psychology
entry.summary: Explore applied social psychology — self-fulfilling prophecy in education, cognitive dissonance in therapy, persuasion in marketing, behavior change in public policy, nudging, and social norms interventions.

entry.title: Advanced Topics & Modern Research
entry.summary: Explore advanced social psychology — social neuroscience, behavioral economics, digital behavior, collective action, moral psychology, and the future of the field.

entry.title: Research Methods & Academic Mastery
entry.summary: Master research methods in social psychology — experimental design, hypothesis formulation, statistical analysis, the replication crisis, and writing research papers for academic publication.

entry.title: Practical Applications - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 8
entry.summary: Behavioral psychology isn't just academic—it's a practical toolkit for improving life, work, and health. In this eighth part of our series, we apply everything we've learned to real-world contexts. The best behavioral interventions combine multiple principles: make behavior easy (friction

entry.title: Behavior Change Models - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 6
entry.summary: Behavior change models provide systematic frameworks for understanding why people behave as they do and how to design effective interventions. In this sixth part of our series, we explore the major theoretical models. Behavior change models help identify which factors to target and which

entry.title: Decision-Making Psychology - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 3
entry.summary: Humans are not rational calculators—we use mental shortcuts and are influenced by emotions. In this third part of our series, we explore the psychology behind decision-making. Bounded rationality means we make "good enough" decisions with limited information and cognitive resources—not optimal ones.

entry.title: Foundations of Behavior - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 1
entry.summary: Behavioral psychology studies observable actions and the environmental factors that influence them. In this first part of our comprehensive 11-part series, we'll explore the fundamental concepts that underpin all behavioral science. Behavior is fundamentally the interaction between a person and

entry.title: Habit Formation & Breaking - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 2
entry.summary: Habits are automatic behaviors stored in the basal ganglia. In this second part of our comprehensive 11-part series, we'll explore how habits form, persist, and change. Habits are not about motivation—they're about repeated loops that become automatic through consistent practice.

entry.title: Motivation & Drive - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 4
entry.summary: Motivation is the internal and external force that initiates, guides, and sustains goal-oriented behavior. In this fourth part of our series, we dive deep into the psychology of drive. Motivation is not a fixed trait—it's a dynamic process influenced by goals, rewards, autonomy, and meaning.

entry.title: Behavioral Neuroscience Basics - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 9
entry.summary: Understanding the neuroscience behind behavior gives us deeper insights into why we act the way we do and how to design more effective interventions. In this ninth part of our series, we explore the brain-behavior connection. The brain evolved to conserve energy—habits form because automated

entry.title: Nudge Theory & Choice Architecture: Behavioral Economics in Action
entry.summary: A nudge is any aspect of choice architecture that alters people's behavior predictably without forbidding options or significantly changing economic incentives. In this fifth part of our series, we explore the science of behavioral design. Nudges work by making desired behaviors easier, more

entry.title: Behavioral Research Methods - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 10
entry.summary: Understanding research methods helps you critically evaluate behavioral claims and design your own interventions. In this tenth part of our series, we explore the scientific toolkit of behavioral psychology. Correlation ≠ causation. Only well-designed experiments with random assignment can

entry.title: Social Influence & Persuasion - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 7
entry.summary: Humans are fundamentally social beings—our behaviors, beliefs, and decisions are profoundly shaped by others. In this seventh part of our series, we explore the science of social influence and persuasion. Social influence operates through three main routes: compliance (behavioral change),

entry.title: Applied Behavioral Therapy - Complete Behavioral Psychology Series Part 11
entry.summary: Behavioral therapy applies the principles we've learned throughout this series to clinical treatment. In this final part of our series, we explore evidence-based therapeutic approaches rooted in behavioral psychology. Behavioral therapies focus on changing what people do (behaviors) rather than

entry.title: Behavioral Psychology Assessment
entry.summary: Test your mastery of the Behavioral Psychology series. All questions, no time limit, detailed feedback after each answer. This assessment covers all topics from the Behavioral Psychology series.

entry.title: Cognitive Psychology Assessment
entry.summary: Test your mastery of the Cognitive Psychology series. All questions, no time limit, detailed feedback after each answer. This assessment covers all topics from the Cognitive Psychology series.

entry.title: Social Psychology Assessment
entry.summary: Test your mastery of the Social Psychology series. All questions, no time limit, detailed feedback after each answer. This assessment covers all topics from the Social Psychology series.
